sites.name: Vyazma Railway Depot
sites.role: Locomotive servicing and traction depot on the Moscow–Minsk mainline; key node for westward military rail traffic

strikes.summary: Ukrainian long-range drones ignited fires inside the Vyazma railway depot; OSINT channels Exilenova+ and ASTRA geolocated fires at the depot northeast of the central station and reported hits on the traction power substation supplying rail lines.

sites.notes: Coords city-level; ASTRA geolocation places fire at the rail depot NE of Vyazma central station; traction substation also reportedly struck.

sites.name: Toropets 107th GRAU Arsenal
sites.role: Strategic MoD missile/artillery storage; held Iskander-M, Tochka-U, S-300 missiles, guided bombs and North Korean KN-23 ballistic missiles; ~30,000 t munitions

strikes.summary: Massive Ukrainian drone strike destroyed the 107th GRAU arsenal; 82-metre crater, fires across ~13 km² (NASA satellite), a magnitude-2.8 seismic event, blast felt up to 300 km. ~30,000 t of munitions destroyed; SBU confirmed responsibility.

sites.notes: Facility ~13 km N of Toropets; coords from Wikipedia infobox (56°30′N 31°43′E). One of the largest single-strike munitions destructions of the war.

sites.name: Oktyabrsky 23rd GRAU Arsenal
sites.name_local: 23-й арсенал ГРАУ, с. Октябрьский, Тверская область
sites.role: GRAU strategic arsenal; stored Iskander and Tochka-U ballistic missiles and S-300 SAM munitions; 2.6 km² facility

strikes.summary: Ukrainian fixed-wing drone strike set the northern storage area ablaze; NASA satellites confirmed fires across most of the 2.6 km² facility. Coordinated with the simultaneous Tikhoretsk strike; General Staff confirmed.

sites.notes: Facility-level coords from Militarnyi satellite analysis (56.3592°N 31.6499°E); distinct facility ~50 km S of Toropets.

sites.name: Tikhoretsk Ammunition Depot (719th Artillery Ammunition Base)
sites.role: One of Russia's three largest ammunition depots; southern logistics hub for Iskander missiles, North Korean KN-23 shells, Smerch rockets and artillery ammunition

strikes.summary: Ukrainian drone strike destroyed ~90% of storage capacity; ≥2,000 t of ammunition (incl. North Korean supply) destroyed; a Podlet radar was also hit; 1,200+ residents evacuated. Satellite imagery confirmed near-total destruction.

sites.notes: Facility-level coords from Militarnyi satellite imagery (45.8871°N 40.0422°E, Kamenniy village). Military unit 57229-41.

sites.name: Karachev 67th GRAU Arsenal
sites.role: GRAU strategic arsenal; stored missile/artillery ammunition incl. North Korean KN-23 shells and glide bombs, much in open-air storage; ~114 km from the border

strikes.summary: Overnight Ukrainian drone strike on the 67th GRAU arsenal near Karachev; large fires with secondary detonations confirmed on video. Reported as the second strike on the facility within two weeks; open-air storage amplified the damage.

sites.name: Kerch (Crimean) Bridge
sites.role: 19 km road-rail bridge linking Krasnodar Krai to Crimea; Russia's primary overland supply route for forces in occupied southern Ukraine

strikes.summary: Truck bomb (~22,770 kg explosives) detonated at 06:07; two road spans collapsed into the water, seven rail fuel-tanker wagons set ablaze, five killed; road and rail traffic suspended.
SBU 'Sea Baby' unmanned surface vehicles struck both road and rail sections; two civilians killed, traffic temporarily halted. Ukraine publicly claimed responsibility and released video.
SBU claimed a months-long covert underwater mining operation; 1,100 kg of explosives detonated against underwater bridge pillars at 04:44, which the SBU said 'severely damaged' the supports.

sites.notes: Coords from Wikipedia infobox (45.3011°N 36.5125°E). Three confirmed Ukrainian strikes 2022–2025; bridge remains partially operational.

sites.name: Chonhar Bridge
sites.role: Road bridge on the R-280 highway crossing the Syvash lagoon; primary land supply route linking occupied Crimea to occupied Kherson/Zaporizhzhia

strikes.summary: Ukraine confirmed a Storm Shadow cruise-missile strike on the Chonhar Bridge; Russia claimed 12 fired/7 downed, proxy Saldo acknowledged infrastructure damage. Follows a June 22 2023 strike.

Ukraine's Code 9.2 unit and 1st Separate Assault Battalion struck the bridge deck with FP-2 Fire Point and long-range Behemoth drones; Planet Labs/RFE-RL Schemes imagery confirmed burn marks. A pontoon bridge was installed as a replacement.

Second drone strike within 48 hours rendered the bridge completely impassable; Ukraine's CCD declared it 'destroyed'. Russian-installed Saldo claimed 25 drones intercepted but acknowledged traffic suspended.

sites.notes: Coords settlement-level (Chonhar ~46.0°N 34.45°E); bridge spans the Syvash lagoon. Declared destroyed June 2026 with a pontoon replacement.

sites.name: Dzhankoi Rail Junction & Depot (Crimea Gateway)
sites.role: Primary railway gateway into occupied Crimea; hub for ammunition, fuel and troop transfers from the Russian mainland to the southern front

strikes.summary: Explosions at an ammunition depot near Maiske (~10 km from Dzhankoi) damaged ~10 km of railway between Azovske and Rozizd; seven passenger trains failed to reach destinations, ~2,000 residents evacuated. Russian MoD blamed 'sabotage'.

Ukrainian drones struck the Dzhankoi checkpoint and a railway bridge in the June 2026 Crimea logistics-interdiction campaign; traffic toward the checkpoint halted and two Russian locomotives on the Crimean line were also struck (Defence Express video).

sites.notes: Coords city-level (Dzhankoi). A March 2023 strike that destroyed Kalibr missiles in rail transit is also attributed to this node.

sites.name: Chapayevka River Railway Bridge
sites.name_local: Железнодорожный мост через р. Чапаевка, Самарская область
sites.role: Railway bridge on the line serving the Polimer explosives plant in Chapayevsk; carries military ammunition from factory to front; ~1,000 km from the frontline

strikes.summary: Support structures of the railway bridge over the Chapayevka River were blown up ~06:00; HUR (Defense Intelligence) confirmed the operation and said the bridge would be impassable 'for weeks'. ~1,000 km from the frontline.

sites.notes: Coords city-level (Chapayevsk ~52.98°N 49.71°E). HUR cited the Polimer JSC explosives plant as the strategic target served by this line.

sites.name: Melitopol Locomotive Depot
sites.role: Locomotive servicing depot in occupied Melitopol; central shop holds locomotives moving ammunition and equipment; key southern-front rail logistics hub

strikes.summary: Ukraine struck the Melitopol locomotive depot; explosions, fires and power outages across the city. Mayor Ivan Fedorov said it was the third strike on a railway depot that week.
A HIMARS strike hit the depot's central shop and the electrical substation powering the rail line; six wounded, power outages across parts of Melitopol.

sites.notes: Coords city-level (Melitopol). HIMARS/GMLRS confirmed for the April 2 strike.
